They would get found out that they were novices and not that smart and that they dropped out of high school. You get, they were, they, they were being found out. When I invented the straight line, what it did, it was a sort of end to end system so that you know exactly what you were going to say before you set it the whole way through. In other words, it was a fully inclusive. I don't mean just rebuttals because like, you know, rebuttals are not like, when I say, you know, answer anyone's objection, it's not like rebuttals. Rebuttals just give you the right to speak more. It was the actual language patterns themselves, these, these independent language patterns I created for them. And then showing that a hook it together along a straight line to make every sale the same because here's the deal. Every sale is different without the straight line. See, when I said to them, guys, every sale is the same. Don't you get it? That's cause I was making it the same. And when I said, watch the straight line, what I did there was I said, watch, here's how I do it. I corral it within these boundaries, right? So while they have different needs, prospects, different values, different pain points, different budgets, different experiences they bring into any situation of influence, right? All those things are different and they're good to say different things. So in that sense, yeah, every sale is different. But by using the straight line system, you can make every sale the same. And remember, it's not exactly the same because a straight line represents the perfect sale with no objections, no interruptions, a lay down. But that's not the way it goes. Typically we have these spaces above and below the line inside the boundaries, right? And that's where healthy influence occurs. So it's not like you're really on the straight line. You're off the straight line, but inside the boundaries and moving forward towards the close. And because we know there are certain core elements that have to line up in every sale, we can then make every sale the same by taking control, by taking control of the sale right out of the gate. And then we slowly but surely line up these elements. However, in the absence of knowing exactly what you're going to say, it's impossible to use the straight line at the highest level. Yeah, you can use some of the tonality, some biolanguage, right? Some basic stuff and that's why some good enough factors where you can just get a pop of 20, 30%. But when I say to close anyone who's closeable, I'm talking about a very different type of situation. I'm talking about a different level of application of the straight line. And that level requires you to have kick scripts. Doesn't matter whether you sell in person or over the phone. When I say scripted, the broken language patterns. In other words, you have to know what you're going to say before you enter an encounter. And I don't care what you sell. You can do that in any industry offline onto the matter. But here's the point. In the absence of having perfectly constructed scripts, which is why I spoke about the script bill the last week, it was such a huge deal, right? In the absence of perfectly constructed scripts, what happens is you spend so much time trying to think about the right things to say that you can't focus on what you're prospering saying back to you. Picking up on the cues of by language and tonality to figure out where they are on a certain scale for each of the three 10s. To pick up on their unconscious cues, they're letting off at where they feel what they're thinking, right? Let me tell you a story here. There's a video circulating around the internet of me closing a really interesting sale in the dairy industry. And you probably know the story. I probably told you where I closed the sale. It was being staged. It was at a psychology company, right? It was a psychology. They paid me 50 grand a day. It was back about 10 years ago to sit in the room and go through all sorts of tests. They wanted to dissect what was in my DNA that made my ability to sell just so extreme. How did I close at your high level and teach others to do the same with the straight line? So they tested me. Everything from Rorschach, Inklot test to what's this association, what do you think when you see that? What are your characters of this? How do you feel about this? And they also did what are called critical incident scenarios. Well, they actually set me up, filmed it. They said, I want you to, they called actors in. This guy's going to pretend he owns a fur coat. He's coming to buy a fur coat. Sell him a fur coat. Like, sell me this pen, right? Another guy comes in and you get to sell the guy if he's like wholesale for a machinery, right? Another guy is travel packages. And one of them was in a financial product, was selling them on opening up an account to manage money, right? And I said, all right, fair enough. So they start the exercise off by handing me a dossier about the eighth, about 10, 12 pages, right? And if you have 15 minutes to go through this dossier, it talks about this company, fictitious company, right? The CEO who is going to be an actor is going to walk in the industry itself and self worth. And then we want you to close this CEO on opening up an account with you to manage money. So I said, all right, fair. If I start writing, I look up looking through the material, right? About 10 minutes and I start writing my script. They come in 15 minutes later and say, you're ready? I said, well, can I have a bit longer? I said, yeah, take as long as you want. I said, all right, thanks. 20 minutes later, they come in. I'm still working. They said, you're done. I said, oh, almost. Can you give me another 20, 30? Yeah, take your time. Great. I kept writing. Finally, an hour and a half later, an hour and 20 later, they finally come in. You ready? I said, yep, now I'm ready. Now, the guy comes in. He's an actor, but it's all filmed with lights and everything. It's very professional, right? And we start going through the sale and I'll actually attach the link so you can see this. It's very interesting, right? Through my whole straight line sale with this guy and it's, I'm sounding awesome. Okay, I'm doing, you know, I'm good at what I do, right? And we get to the end and he's giving me a lot of objections, right? He's not a lay down by any stretch. He's being really tough for guy. I'm just plowing through it, doing my thing. And finally, I get the guy really boxed in with his belief systems, the whole thing. I'm doing a really good job, right? And the guy's like, I asked him for the order for the fifth time. He's like, fine, I'll do it. And he starts cracking up and all the psychologists who were in the background, they were cool. They were cracking up. I'm like, what's the joke? Well, I closed, they said, we told the guy that under no circumstances, he allowed to say yes. He wasn't allowed to close. He was a no, you know, don't buy. And I still gotten to buy. And the guy's like, you were just too persuasive. So, but here's the point, the interesting thing. They said, we've tested 50 other sales will top producers. We want to get a baseline before we tested you. 50 people. No one closed the guy. And not one of the 50 spent more than five minutes reviewing the material. How about that? They didn't even review the material top producers. I spent almost an hour and a half and I closed the person. Now, obviously, yes, I have natural sales skills, right? And I know the straight line system and I'm a top producer, one of the best in the world. But what kind of edge did I have? I had 90 minutes of preparation that I built. I wrote all my patterns, everything perfect. So when the guy came in, I knew exactly what I was going to say. I had confidence that came across. I was clear. I could read his body language. Just tone out. How you can't use the straight line. You can't close at any reasonable level unless you get your patterns down. So I want this to be script week. I want you to go back for your scripts and make sure they're perfect. Make sure that they're usable. I mean, they're smooth as silk. They make sense. Remember, we are in the business of creating scream from the hilltops airtight logical cases for all three tents. In the business of creating scream from the hilltops, airtight emotional cases for the three tents. We need to lower the action threshold and on paint. These things must be prepared before you walk in the door or before you pick up the phone. Not every word, but your core patterns. And then you link them together using the tactics of the straight line system. In the absence of that, you are literally, it's like almost like you're trying to win a hundred yard dash with one foot tied up. Seriously.
